heading: Overseas public diplomacy grants
label: 1475h Overseas public diplomacy grants
notes: References in TextThe Mutual Educational and Cultural Exchange Act of 1961, referred to in subsec. CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Foreign Relations Authorization Act, Fiscal Years 1992 and 1993, and not as part of the United States Information and Educational Exchange Act of 1948 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Competitive grant procedures
children.label: a Competitive grant procedures
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), the Department of State shall work to achieve full and open competition in the award of grants for carrying out its overseas public diplomacy functions.

such a grant is made under the Mutual Educational and Cultural Exchange Act of 1961 (commonly known as the Fulbright-Hays Act) [ et seq.] or any statute which expressly authorizes or requires that a grant be made with a specified entity;22 U.S.C. 2451

the terms of an international agreement or treaty between the United States Government and a foreign government or international organization have the effect of requiring the use of procedures other than competitive procedures;
a recipient organization has developed particular expertise in the planning and administration of longstanding exchange programs important to United States foreign policy; or

After , overseas public diplomacy grants awarded by the Department of State shall substantially comply with Department of State grant guidelines and applicable circulars of the Office of Management and Budget.October 1, 1991

If the Agency determines that a grantee has not satisfied the requirement of paragraph (1), the Department of State shall notify the grantee of the suspension of payments under a grant unless compliance is achieved within 90 days of such notice.1So in original. Probably should be “Department”.1
The Agency shall suspend payments under any such grant which remains in noncompliance 90 days after notification under paragraph (2).1
